mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns through anticipated revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in 65% of co-occurring months, or the interaction term loses predictive power when added to a baseline macro model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during low inflation periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70% of months with CPI acceleration above 0.4%, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal strong labor demand and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000 in a month
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in 55% of high-payroll months, or the predictive relationship vanishes when controlling for prior month trends.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates a synchronized economic upturn, reinforcing earnings growth expectations and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The co-movement signal fails to predict positive returns in 60% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.
